channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Justin Hemmes installs four-metre noise barriers at Club Rose Bay beer garden amid ongoing complaints
item.description: Hospitality entrepreneur Justin Hemmes has installed four-metre-high noise-muffling plastic walls around the beer garden at Club Rose Bay in Sydney’s eastern suburbs. The structures line the perimeter...

item.title: Markets Brace for Impact as Trump’s Deadline for Iran Looms
item.description: Global markets are facing heightened uncertainty as the deadline imposed by US President Donald Trump on Iran approaches. Trump has demanded that Iran end its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz by 8pm W...

item.title: Nigeria allocates N1.475trn to agriculture in 2026 budget to support food security
item.description: Nigeria don increase the budget for agriculture for 2026, as the Federal Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security dey get N1.475 trillion. This one show small improvement from the N1.24 trillion wey ...

item.title: Fuel supply challenges in Nakuru push public transport costs higher
item.description: A serious fuel shortage has hit Nakuru County, causing matatu operators to increase fares for commuters travelling to different parts of the region. Many petrol stations have run dry over the past few...

item.title: Evacuated Indian Fisherfolk from Iran Reach Chennai Safely via Armenia
item.description: A total of 345 Indian fisherfolk, primarily from Tamil Nadu, reached Chennai on Saturday evening after being evacuated from Iran through Armenia amid the intensifying conflict in West Asia. These indi...

item.title: US and Iran Agree on Temporary Ceasefire Amid Escalating Tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran on April 7, 2026, just hours before his deadline for Tehran to reopen the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz. The ceasefire, mediat...

item.title: Israel and US Strike Tehran Synagogue as Tensions Escalate in Middle East
item.description: As the deadline for US President Donald Trump’s ultimatum to Iran nears, US and Israeli forces have escalated their airstrikes against Tehran, including an attack that has 'completely destroyed' the R...

item.title: Moi Mbiruri School Play Fails to Qualify for Nationals, Not Banned Over Singapore Reference
item.description: A drama play by Moi High School Mbiruri in Embu has sparked debate after it failed to qualify for the 2026 Kenya National Drama and Film Festival. The play, titled 'The Gap,' follows a motivational sp...

item.title: House Ways and Means Committee Holds Hearing on Proposed Bayanihan 3 Relief Package for Oil and Energy Crisis
item.description: On April 8, 2026, the House of Representatives Ways and Means Committee, chaired by Rep. Miro Quimbo, convened the first in a series of hearings to address the ongoing oil and energy crisis affecting ...

item.title: Trump Postpones Iran Action Amidst Escalating Tensions
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has temporarily halted planned military action against Iran, a decision made in the wake of increasingly alarming threats emanating from the region. According to reports fr...

item.title: Gachagua launches DCP candidate for Emurua Dikirr by-election, expressing confidence in Narok political gains
item.description: Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has announced the candidature of a Democratic Conservative Party (DCP) representative ahead of the Emurua Dikirr mini poll in Narok County. Speaking during a public f...

item.title: One-Punch Attack Victim's Family Worried Compensation Won't Last
item.description: The family of Craig Lewis-Williams, a 50-year-old man left severely disabled following a single punch in November 2021, are expressing serious concerns about the adequacy of his compensation. Craig, w...

item.title: James Hird defends Essendon captain Zach Merrett and his wife amid social media backlash
item.description: Former Essendon legend James Hird has come to the defence of the club’s captain, Zach Merrett, and Merrett’s wife, Alexandra, following online criticism over a TikTok video. During Essendon’s loss to ...

item.title: Trump and Iran Agree on Two-Week Ceasefire, Strait of Hormuz to Reopen
item.description: United States President Donald Trump on Tuesday announced a temporary two-week ceasefire in West Asia in return for Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Iran confirmed the ceasefire, with Foreign Mini...

item.title: Supreme Court Examines Impact of Unprogrammed Funds in National Budget
item.description: The Supreme Court recently held oral arguments concerning the national budget, with a particular focus on unprogrammed appropriations (UA). Budget experts invited to the proceedings raised concerns th...

item.title: Pakistan Tries Last-Minute Diplomacy to Prevent US Strike on Iran
item.description: In a tense global standoff, Pakistan has made a last-minute bid to avert a catastrophic US strike on Iran. Hours before President Donald Trump’s deadline, which he ominously warned could lead to the d...

item.title: Hundreds Arrested During Easter Road Safety Operations, Including Drunk Driving Incidents
item.description: During the 2026 Easter holiday period, South African law enforcement made significant arrests in efforts to curb crime and improve road safety. Nearly 15,000 people were apprehended across the country...

item.title: Saskatchewan Commemorates the Humboldt Broncos Tragedy on Green Shirt Day
item.description: On April 7, 2026, Saskatchewan observed the 8th annual Green Shirt Day, an event dedicated to remembering the victims of the 2018 Humboldt Broncos bus crash. The day honours those affected by the trag...

item.title: Amit Shah condemns Kharge's controversial remarks on BJP and RSS
item.description: New Delhi: On Tuesday, Union Home Minister Amit Shah strongly condemned Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge for his recent remarks comparing the BJP and RSS to a venomous snake. Shah called Kharge's sta...

item.title: Australian Shares and US Markets React to Temporary US-Iran Truce
item.description: Australian shares are expected to open higher following a late rebound on Wall Street, as investors responded to news that US President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week extension on his deadline to I...
